text: Last class you ran the full interview and you got the senior role.
Now you lead the team, and the process is documented by you.

The report is reviewed every Friday.
Tickets are assigned at the stand-up.
The build was tested before launch.
The runbook was written by the whole team.
Hi, Rafael. It is my first day. How does the release procedure work here?
Welcome. It is simple. Every change is documented in the wiki, and tickets are assigned at the stand-up.
And before we ship? How is the build checked and approved?
The build is tested by the quality team, and it is approved only when every check passes. Then it is monitored for two days.
That is very clear. And who maintains the runbook?
It is maintained by everyone, not just me. Last quarter the process was standardized across the squads, so nothing depends on one person.
Every change is documented in the wiki before it goes live.
Tickets are assigned at the stand-up, and the work is monitored all week.
The build is tested by the quality team and approved when every check passes.
Last quarter the process was standardized across all the squads.
The runbook is maintained by everyone, so nothing depends on one person.

Welcome to the team. Let me walk you through how we work, so nothing depends on one person. First, every change is documented in our wiki before it goes live. The procedure is simple and it is followed by everyone. Tickets are assigned at the Monday stand-up, and progress is monitored on the board all week. The build is tested by the quality team, and it is only approved when every check passes. After release, the system is monitored for forty-eight hours. Last quarter this process was standardized across all three squads, and our incidents were cut in half. The runbook was written by the whole team, so it is maintained by everyone, not just me. If you follow the steps, you will never feel lost. Everything you need is documented, and the handover is designed so the work continues without me in the room.

Hi Rafael, this is Sofia from the program office. Great news about your first month as lead. The leadership team reviewed your handover document, and it was praised in this morning's meeting. They said the process is clear and the runbook is well maintained. One quick request: the new vendor onboarding will be standardized next quarter, and your template was chosen as the model. Could you record a short walkthrough so it can be shared with the other squads? It does not need to be long. The deadline is Friday, and the file should be uploaded to the shared drive. Thank you for setting such a high bar. Your work is noticed, and people are already asking to be assigned to your team. Talk soon.

First, the change is documented in the wiki before anything goes live. Next, the ticket is assigned to an owner at the stand-up. Then the work is monitored on the board all week. After that, the build is tested by the quality team and approved only when every check passes. Finally, the new process is standardized across the squads, so it is maintained by everyone.
